author: bharath.vedartham
content:
hey <@1479601656206327970> couple of thoughts:
1. Sidecars would ideally be optional with ePBS unlike today where we require them. Having a default option in clients to query multple builders for bids and allowing operators to have sidecars if they desire seems reasonable to me. A hard requirement on sidecars to participate in the PBS market has historically caused a lot of issues related to maintanence, mev-boost ownership and hard fork testing which we can try to avoid with ePBS by making them optional. 
2. The p2p auction likely wont have meaningful bids. Builders might use it to set a reserve price in the auction but i expect most of the meaningful bids to come from the builder-api. 
3. One of the biggest blockers for sidecars which we have discussed with them so far is requiring proposers to sign the request to get the bid, as then the sidecar would need access to keys. I think we can make request signing optional and operators who want to use sidecars can choose not to send auth. 
4. I would assume sidecars don't listen to the public auction, i guess they could by connecting to the `execution_payload_bid` subnet, that shouldn't be too hard. They can just connect to the beacon node peer and get the bids forwarded from the beacon node.
